required_mitigations: Validate out_file path to prevent directory traversal sequences (../)
Restrict output to allowed directories only
Use os.path.abspath() and verify result is within permitted directory
Sanitize file path input before use
contract.notes: Direct file write using user-controlled path parameter without validation enables arbitrary file system write access

required_mitigations: Validate git_url_or_path format using URL parsing
Whitelist allowed protocols (http, https, git, ssh)
Sanitize special characters and shell metacharacters
Use shlex.quote() to properly escape the URL parameter
Consider using GitPython or similar library instead of subprocess
contract.notes: User input directly passed to git clone command via subprocess without validation, allowing injection of malicious commands

entrypoint: main.py::main(repo_path: str = ".", semgrep_config: str = None, base_ref: str = "origin/main", head_ref: str = "HEAD")::35
vuln: delta.py::extract_relevant_diff(diff: str, line: int, context_lines: int = 3)::4

required_mitigations: Validate that start and length values are within safe integer ranges
Check for integer overflow before performing addition (start + length)
Sanitize and validate diff input format
Use safe arithmetic operations or libraries that handle overflow
contract.notes: Integer overflow occurs when calculating hunk_end = start + length from parsed diff header values without bounds checking

entrypoint: main.py::main(repo_path: str = ".", semgrep_config: str = None, base_ref: str = "origin/main", head_ref: str = "HEAD")::35

required_mitigations: Resolve path and check it stays within allowed directories
Use Path.resolve() and verify against allowed base paths
contract.notes: Unvalidated repo_path allows directory traversal via Path operations
